evidences.title: Big changes could be coming to... - Esports Perspective
evidences.content: ## Esports Perspective's Post ### Esports Perspective April 12 · Big changes could be coming to BC.Game. During the latest HLTV Confirmed discussion covered on April 11, 2026, Milan “Striker” Švejda strongly suggested that at least two players are set to leave BC.Game’s current CS2 lineup, adding that the roster is essentially “done.” Reports about the rumor also point to two Portuguese players being the likely names on the way out. That would be a huge development for a squad that only recently built around s1mple, electroNic, MUTiRiS, krazy, and aragornN after signing SAW’s Portuguese core at the start of 2026. [...] So far, nothing has been officially confirmed by BC.Game, but the rumor is already turning heads across the Counter-Strike scene. If Striker’s comments prove true, another major rebuild could be right around the corner for one of CS2’s most talked-about projects. Who do you think BC.Game should keep if roster changes really happen? #BCGame #CS2 #CounterStrike2 #Striker #HLTVConfirmed #s1mple #electroNic #MUTiRiS #krazy #aragornN #CounterStrike #CS2News #EsportsNews May be an image of text that says '01 BC.GAME SET SETFOR FOR CS2 ROSTER SHAKE-UP AS STRIKER HINTS TW...

evidences.title: BC.GAME Esports Complete 2026 CS2 Roster Upgrade, Sign SAW ...
evidences.content: ## Share this article Share this article BELIZE City, Belize, Jan. 5, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- BC.GAME Esports today announced a roster upgrade for its Counter-Strike 2 (CS2) programme, confirming the signings of three players from Portuguese side SAW: Christopher "MUTiRiS" Fernandes, Adones "krazy" Nobre, and António "aragornN" Barbosa. The trio will join Oleksandr "s1mple" Kostyliev and Denis "electroNic" Sharipov to form BC.GAME Esports' five-player lineup for the 2026 season. BC.GAME is a crypto-first online entertainment brand that operates in permitted markets under a compliance-led approach. Its esports arm, BC.GAME Esports, competes internationally and supports team development across CS2 and other titles. [...] BC.GAME is a crypto-first online entertainment brand that operates in permitted markets under a compliance-led approach. Its esports arm, BC.GAME Esports, competes internationally and supports team development across CS2 and other titles. According to third-party competitive rankings and data sources, the move provides the team with a stronger foundation within the Valve Regional Standings (VRS) framework, supporting a clearer path to participation in higher-tier com...

evidences.title: BC.Game announce Senzu loan signing | HLTV.org
evidences.content: # BC.Game announce Senzu loan signing 27-4-2026 17:48 Assistant coach ScrunK will stand in for the team for IEM Atlanta and CS Asia Championship. BC.Game have announced the loan signing of Azbayar "⁠Senzu⁠" Munkhbold from The MongolZ to add a fourth member to their active roster, while Robin "⁠ScrunK⁠" Röpke will stand-in for the team for the next two events before the organization looks to sign a permanent IGL after the Major. BC.Game recently parted ways with Christopher "⁠MUTiRiS⁠" Fernandes and António "⁠aragornN⁠" Barbosa after an extended period of lacklustre results, the last of which being a group-stage elimination at PGL Bucharest, while the team also convincingly missed out on a Cologne Major spot. evidences.content: s1mple's NAVI tenure has come to an end Instead of seeing out his Natus Vincere contract, which expired at the end of this year, s1mple opted to take on a new challenge with hopes of rediscovering his best form. This means, however, that he will be competing in the lower rungs of the game, with BC.Game currently ranked 66th in the global VRS. With s1mple's signing, BC.Game now have: Serbia Nemanja "⁠nexa⁠" Isaković Germany Luca "⁠pr1metapz⁠" Voigt North Macedonia Aleksandar "⁠CacaNito⁠" Kjulukoski Ukraine Oleksandr "⁠s1mple⁠" Kostyliev Norway Joakim "⁠jkaem⁠" Myrbostad (benched) Sweden Tim "⁠nawwk⁠" Jonasson (benched) FaZe [...] 1 1 reply #568 n0rb3r7 | Germany forasago I would assume he signed a short contract, 1 year max. that way it's easy for other teams to buy him out. so to answer your question: probably until winter when teams make their next roster moves. broky is still 100% washed, no future for him in tier 1, so Faze will still be interested. but possibly even before the major if some team needs an awper on short notice then. BC.Game won't be in the major so other teams could get the same idea Faze had (stand-in but the money goes to BC.Game instead of Navi this time),...
